sample_text.ppt/slides/slide1.xml: NEW BETTING ODDS FEATURES Bakery-Audit Odds Automation A focused walkthrough of the newer moneyline, over/under, and spread analysis features. Built from the repository’s odds parsing, pairing, ranking, and Discord review flow. FOCUS Only the newer odds features - Moneyline pairing + ranking - O/U scenario-window logic
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide2.xml: WHY THE ODDS FLOW MATTERS The newer work moves Bakery-Audit from logging bets to comparing odds opportunities. Before the newer odds work - Primary flow was screenshot extraction plus confirmation logging. - Great for record-keeping, but not built for comparing sportsbooks. - No dedicated totals or spread recommendation logic. After the newer odds work - `@bot odds [real|bonus|both]` triggers a dedicated automation flow.
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide3.xml: ODDS WORKFLOW The new odds flow has a clear input -> pairing -> ranking -> output sequence. 1 - User sends `@bot odds real|bonus|both` with screenshots. 2 - Gemini extracts `OddsCandidate` rows with market, site, odds, and line fields. 3 - Models normalize dates, team codes, sites, totals, and spreads.
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide4.xml: NEW FEATURE: MONEYLINE Moneyline now has dedicated pairing, workbook-style calculations, and ranking. - Gemini extracts rows as `market="moneyline"`. - `build_clean_rows(...)` groups opposite sides of the same matchup on the same date. - `_pick_best_site_pair(...)` rejects same-site pairs and chooses the best cross-site direction. - Moneyline uses workbook-style formulas for hedge, profit, ROI, and rake. - Bonus mode now blocks Cloudbet as the bonus side. CODE-BACKED EXAMPLE
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide5.xml: NEW FEATURE: OVER / UNDER Totals bets are evaluated as score windows instead of a simple win/lose pair. - Gemini extracts totals as `total_over` / `total_under` with `total_line`. - The bot pairs Over and Under across different sites for the same game. - `_optimize_ou_hedge_stake(...)` maximizes the worst-case outcome. - `_ou_profit_outcomes(...)` evaluates buckets below, at, between, and above the lines. - If only same-site pairs exist, the review embed explains why there is no pick. CODE-BACKED EXAMPLE
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide6.xml: NEW FEATURE: SPREAD Spread bets add margin-based pairing and line-window analysis. - Gemini extracts `market="spread"` plus signed `spread_line`. - The bot pairs opposite spread sides across sites for the same matchup. - `_optimize_spread_hedge_stake(...)` solves for the strongest downside protection. - `_spread_profit_outcomes(...)` checks outcomes around the spread thresholds. - Spread results can expose middle-margin windows when two lines leave room in between. CODE-BACKED EXAMPLE
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide7.xml: RANKING + OUTPUT CHANGES The recent work also improved recommendation quality and the final Discord presentation. - `select_top_recommendations(...)` now skips games already used by earlier metric buckets. - Site attribution is preserved more aggressively per screenshot and can backfill from image names. - Rake / edge sign was aligned to the workbook display: `1 - implied probability sum`. - Moneyline bonus instructions are suppressed when the bet site is Cloudbet. Visible product change - Old: multi-page odds result view
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide8.xml: EXAMPLE END-TO-END One odds request can produce reviewable outputs for all three new markets. INPUT - Screenshot A: TOR/CLE moneyline - Screenshot B: TOR/CLE total - Screenshot C: MIN/DAL spread - User sends `@bot odds both` PROCESSING
sample_text.ppt/slides/slide9.xml: TECHNICAL HIGHLIGHTS / IMPACT The newer odds features are the strongest product step-change in the repository. - Strict JSON odds prompt keeps extraction structured. - `OddsCandidate` makes market, site, total, and spread fields explicit. - Cross-site pairing prevents weak same-site comparisons. - Per-market optimization gives each bet type its own logic instead of one generic ranking rule. - The new Discord summary makes the results presentation-ready. 3 markets
